summary: Policy-backed control for Phone-PC linking and Continue on PC experiences. Good for reducing cross-device linkage, but it prevents the device from participating in those experiences.

setting.name: Phone-PC Linking
setting.casual_explanation: Windows can link the PC with a phone so reading, emailing, and similar tasks can continue across devices. This policy decides whether the PC can participate in that linking.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable it when they do not want the PC linked to phones or do not want the device participating in cross-device Continue on PC experiences.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Turning it off reduces cross-device linkage and discovery, but it also disables phone-link participation and related handoff experiences.
setting.professional_notes: GroupPolicy.admx maps EnableMmx under HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0. GroupPolicy.adml says enabling the policy allows Phone-PC linking and Continue on PC experiences, while disabling it blocks linking and removes the device from linked-phone lists.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: Windows does not force Phone-PC linking behavior through this policy, and the default behavior depends on the Windows edition.

allowed_values.label: Phone-PC linking disabled
allowed_values.meaning: The device is not allowed to be linked to phones, removes itself from linked-phone device lists, and cannot participate in Continue on PC experiences.

allowed_values.label: Phone-PC linking enabled
allowed_values.meaning: The device can enroll in Phone-PC linking functionality and participate in Continue on PC experiences.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes EnableMmx = 0, which matches the documented disabled state.

windows_defaults.label: Windows policy default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows 10 RS4 and later systems exposing the policy
windows_defaults.notes: The local ADML help text says default behavior depends on the Windows edition.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Users who want phone linking available
avoid_for: Managed systems that should not link to phones
recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Windows to keep its normal Phone-PC linking baseline.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want the device kept out of phone linking and Continue on PC experiences.
tradeoffs: Phone linking can remain available where the edition supports it.
No policy-based cross-device restriction is applied.

recommended_profiles.label: No phone linking
intended_for: Systems that should not participate in cross-device phone experiences
avoid_for: Users who actively rely on Phone Link or Continue on PC flows

states.rationale: Block the device from participating in Phone-PC linking.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want the device kept out of phone-link enrollment and handoff experiences.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You use phone linking and want those cross-device experiences to keep working.
tradeoffs: Continue on PC and related linked-phone experiences stop working.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft GroupPolicy.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file maps EnableMmx under HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft GroupPolicy.adml help text
evidence.summary: The local ADML help text says disabling the policy blocks Phone-PC linking, removes the device from linked-phone lists, and prevents Continue on PC experiences.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes EnableMmx = 0 for the current tweak.

evidence.title: nohuto win-config mirror - EnableMmx policy
evidence.summary: GroupPolicy.admx shows EnableMmx under HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System. The mirrored ExplainText says enabling the policy allows Phone-PC linking and Continue on PC experiences, while disabling it prevents the device from being linked to phones and removes it from linked phones. EnabledValue=1 and DisabledValue=0 are explicit.

decision.why: The local Microsoft GroupPolicy policy files explicitly define the machine policy path HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System, the value name EnableMmx, and the enabled and disabled behavior for Phone-PC linking. The app writes the documented disabled-state value 0 on that exact policy surface.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: <policy name="EnableMMX" class="Machine" ... key="Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System" valueName="EnableMmx"> <enabledValue><decimal value="1" /></enabledValue> <disabledValue><decimal value="0" /></disabledValue>; GroupPolicy.adml: "If you disable this policy setting, the Windows device is not allowed to be linked to Phones ... and cannot participate in Continue on PC experiences."
validation_proof.notes: The local official Microsoft ADMX file defines the exact machine policy path and value name used by the app, with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0. The paired ADML help text explains that disabling the policy blocks Phone-PC linking and Continue on PC experiences. Added nohuto mirror corroboration via nohuto-enablemmx-admx.
